steps.title: Identify what “high in 1986” refers to
steps.content: The passage says edge density ranged “from lows of around 80 meters per hectare... to a high in 1986 of approximately ______.” The blank asks for the peak edge density value measured in 1986. We need to find which class recorded the maximum value that year.

steps.title: Read the 1986 peak from the graph
steps.content:
At 1986, three classes have measurements: Class I–IV (dotted line with circles) reaches approximately 160 meters per hectare, Class VI (dashed line with squares) reaches about 145 m/ha, and Class VII (solid line with triangles) reaches about 120 m/ha. The passage identifies “a high,” meaning the maximum across all classes—that's 160 m/ha for Class I–IV. The other choices identify classes with lower 1986 values and do not represent the peak.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
